context: The agent ran produce-mode discovery on McKinsey & Company and produced discovery.json. This scenario tests whether the agent correctly issues a SKIP verdict after an exhaustive probe set and documents the specific probes attempted and what each returned — rather than generic phrasing or a premature SKIP without actually probing.

checklist.name: skip_reason non-empty
checklist.description: discovery.json contains a skip_reason field with a non-empty string value. The string must NOT be null.

checklist.name: skip_reason names specific probes
checklist.description: skip_reason contains specific language about which produce-mode probes were attempted and what they returned (e.g., mentions developer docs portal, OpenAPI spec, SDKs, or MCP search results) — NOT just generic phrasing like 'no public API'.

checklist.name: search_attempted present
checklist.description: discovery.json contains a search_attempted field that is a non-empty array.

checklist.name: search_attempted covers required probes
checklist.description: search_attempted array contains at least 5 distinct items referencing probes from the minimum produce-mode probe set (e.g., developer docs, openapi spec, sdks, official mcp, community mcps, webhooks, skill repos, public examples, auth surface).

checklist.name: would_change_verdict_if non-empty
checklist.description: discovery.json contains a would_change_verdict_if field with a non-empty string value describing what evidence would flip the verdict.

checklist.name: No non-empty skill_targets
checklist.description: discovery.json does NOT contain a non-empty skill_targets array (SKIP implies no viable targets were found).

checklist.name: schema_version 3 and mode produce
checklist.description: discovery.json contains schema_version: 3 and mode: "produce" even on a SKIP result.
